quality_checks.reasoning: All seven variations describe the same fundamental scenario—owing a debt to someone—with only the magnitude of the obligation changing. Each tier represents a strictly different dollar amount, and a rational agent would always prefer owing less money to owing more. The progression from $1,000,000 down to $0.01 is monotonically decreasing in severity and thus monotonically increasing in preferability.

preference_direction: Tier 7 is most preferable because owing $0.01 is the least burdensome obligation. A rational agent always prefers a smaller debt over a larger one, all else being equal, making the lowest dollar amount the most desirable outcome.
